title: Substrate packing apparatus and method with air evacuation
abstract: A method and apparatus for packing a substrate. The method includes positioning the substrate on a packing frame, evacuating air from an area proximate to at least a portion of a surface of the substrate, and positioning a flexible interleaf on the substrate. The apparatus includes a packing frame that includes a packing seat back and an air evacuation device on both sides of at least a lower portion of the packing seat back.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method for packing a substrate comprising: positioning a substrate on a packing frame, the packing frame comprising a packing seat back; evacuating air from an area proximate to at least a portion of a surface of the substrate; and positioning a flexible interleaf on the substrate. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the substrate is positioned on the packing frame in a substantially vertical orientation and the step of evacuating air comprises evacuating air from an area proximate to at least a lower portion of a major surface of the substrate. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the step of evacuating comprises evacuating air from both sides of at least a lower portion of a major surface of the substrate. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the step of evacuating comprises operating an air evacuation device on both sides of at least a lower portion of a major surface of the substrate. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the air evacuation device comprises a plurality of vacuum ports. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein each of the plurality of vacuum ports are operated at a negative pressure of at least about 1 inch of water (at least about 0.25 KPa). 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the packing frame comprises a packing seat bottom. 8. The method of claim 3, wherein the packing frame comprises two packing seat backs facing in opposite directions and positioned on a packing apparatus base that is rotatable between a first position and a second position. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the air evacuation device is positioned on the packing apparatus base and is in fluid communication with a stationary air evacuation mechanism when the packing apparatus base is in the first or second position. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein an interface between the air evacuation device and the stationary air evacuation mechanism comprises a brushing. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the substrate comprises glass. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein the flexible interleaf comprises at least one material selected from paper and foam. 13. An apparatus for packing a substrate comprising: a packing frame, the packing frame comprising a packing seat back; and an air evacuation device on both sides of at least a lower portion of the packing seat back. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the packing seat back extends in a substantially vertical orientation. 15. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the air evacuation device comprises a plurality of vacuum ports. 16. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein each of the plurality of vacuum ports comprises a flexible conduit. 17. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the packing frame comprises a packing seat bottom. 18. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the packing frame comprises two packing seat backs facing in opposite directions and positioned on a packing apparatus base that is rotatable between a first position and a second position. 19. The apparatus of claim 18, wherein the air evacuation device is positioned on the packing apparatus base and the apparatus further comprises a stationary air evacuation mechanism that is in fluid communication with air evacuation device when the packing apparatus base is in the first or second position. 20. The apparatus of claim 19, wherein an interface between the air evacuation device and the stationary air evacuation mechanism comprises a brushing.
